text.title: Rollback, Smoke Tests, and DORA Metric Instrumentation
text.summary: Auto-rollback on smoke failure, health check endpoint pattern, DORA event emission, and agent gotchas.

section.title: Auto-Rollback Pattern

rule.statement: Implement rollback as: set image → rollout status (timeout) → smoke test → rollback to previous SHA on any failure; always re-run rollout status after rollback to confirm recovery.
rule.rationale: A rollback that does not confirm recovery may leave the cluster in a broken intermediate state; the confirmation step closes the feedback loop.

section.reference: Bash script implementing set-image, rollout-status, smoke, rollback on failure.

rule.statement: Implement /health (liveness) and /health/ready (readiness) as separate endpoints; readiness checks all dependencies (DB, cache, external APIs) and returns 503 if any fail.
rule.rationale: A liveness probe that checks dependencies kills the pod when a downstream service is slow, causing cascade restarts. Readiness only removes the pod from the load balancer rotation.

rule.statement: Emit deploy_started, deploy_succeeded, deploy_failed events with version, sha, and duration_seconds to a single metrics sink; instrument once at the pipeline gate, not per retry.
rule.rationale: Per-retry instrumentation double-counts retries as deploys, inflating deployment frequency and change failure rate. The gate fires once per intentional deploy.

antipattern.description: Agent uses continue-on-error: true on smoke or rollout steps to make the pipeline "pass".
antipattern.reason: continue-on-error hides a real failure; the pipeline reports success while the deployment is broken. DORA change failure rate becomes meaningless.
